name: apple-notes-blade-mcp
title: Apple Notes
description: On-disk Apple Notes reader — NoteStore.sqlite + protobuf body decode + folder hierarchy. Read-only; never opens the network. Library form factor (DD-240 Phase A) — compiled into Stallari.
tagline: Years of notes, on-device, no iCloud round-trip
author: groupthink-dev

setup.blurb: Read on-disk Notes.app data — folders, notes, search, body content via protobuf decode, attachment metadata. Years of notes on-device that an iCloud-side API can't reach (locally-cached drafts, removed-account archives, pre-iCloud history).
setup.help_text: Local-corpus class blade (DD-240). Library form factor — compiled into Stallari, not a separate subprocess. Requires Full Disk Access on the Stallari daemon binary so it can read ~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.notes/NoteStore.sqlite. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access and enable Stallari.app + the stallari-helper binary inside it.

help.label: Grant Full Disk Access
help.description: Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access. Add /Applications/Stallari.app and /Applications/Stallari.app/Contents/MacOS/stallari-helper, then toggle both on.

help.label: DD-240 — apple-notes-blade-mcp
help.description: Library form factor inside StallariKit (DD-240 invariant #8 — never advertised externally).

prerequisites: Full Disk Access granted to the Stallari daemon binary (stallari-helper) — needed to read ~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.notes/
Notes.app must have been launched at least once (so NoteStore.sqlite exists)

notes: 5 read-only tools (list_folders, list_notes, search_notes, head, read_note). Library form factor (DD-240 Phase A) — compiled into StallariKit via SPM dep on apple-notes-blade-mcp v0.1.0. **Never advertised on the daemon's :9847/mcp HTTP MCP surface per DD-240 invariant #8** — consumed only via the in-process Dispatcher path through AppleNotesBladeWiring. NoteStore.sqlite SQLite reads + protobuf body decode; no AppleScript, no probabilistic inference inside the blade.
